title: Contact - Alan Campbell MP for Tynemouth
text_excerpt: Contact - Alan Campbell MP for Tynemouth Skip to main content Alan Campbell MP for Tynemouth Community Commitment Common Sense Home About Latest News News Guardian Community News Contact Cost of Living Resource Magazine Community Survey Privacy Policy About More Search Search the website Search Menu Contact Alan’s role is to represent you as a constituent within the Tynemouth Constituency, regardless of your politics. Not sure if you’re a constituent? Check here . If you’re concerned about a local or national issue Alan can make representations on your behalf to the government, North Tyneside Council or other agency and will keep you informed about your case. If you would like Alan’s help, please get in touch either by phone, email or post and make sure you provide the following information; Your full name and postal address A contact telephone number Any reference numbers that relate to your case A summary of your problem including any steps you may have already taken to try and resolve it By providing Alan Campbell MP with information about your views, you consent to allowing Alan Campbell MP and his staff to use the information you’ve given, to contact you by email, text, phone and website. You can see the full Privacy Policy here Advice Surgeries My office is open for surgeries and face to face advice by appointment. My office is also available Monday to Friday 9am until 5pm by email and on the telephone. Contact Alan Email: [email protected] Write to: Alan Campbell MP, Houses of Parliament, London SW1A ORS Phone: 0191 2571927 How an MP can help you Members of Parliament are elected to the House of Commons to represent the interests and concerns of all the people who live in their constituency, whether they voted for them at the General Election or not. MPs can only raise concerns on the behalf of their own constituents so please make sure to include your full address in any correspondence. If you are unsure who your MP is you can find out using this site: https://members.parliament.uk/members/commons An MP can also only act on behalf of constituents who contact him directly. This means that if you would like your MP to act, for instance, on behalf of a family member, that family member will need to make contact directly. This is necessary to maintain confidentiality between your MP and the individual constituents who contact him MPs can help you with matters such as: Tax problems involving HM Revenue & Customs (but not issues with, for example, the council tax, which is administered by your local authority). Problems with hospitals and the NHS. Problems with benefits, pensions and national insurance. Issues dealt with by the Government departments. School closures (but not day to day problems involving schools, which are overseen by their governors and North Tyneside local education authority). MPs can raise concerns with the police on behalf of a constituent, but have no role in decision making or law enforcement. MPs cannot help you private disputes with other individuals or with companies. MPs cannot offer legal advice and have no jurisdiction over the police or the courts. Constituents are therefore advised to consult with a solicitor should they need professional advice for legal disputes. They also cannot provide any financial or business advice. An MP is not able to assist in settling family arguments or private disputes with neighbours or employers. MPs do not have jurisdiction over local council decisions or policy. However, they can write to a local council and ask them to look into a problem or to reconsider an issue. In the first instance, constituents should contact their local council or councillor should they have a query concerning a council department. If you object to a planning application or proposed development, then you should first lodge your objections through North Tyneside Council or your local councillor.
